articles.summary: One user query triggers 50 model calls. You suddenly realize your "per request" pricing doesn't make sense. Users get hit with surprise bills, while your usage dashboard is still stuck two hours in the past.

articles.title: AI MARKET CLARITY
articles.summary: Elad Gil argues the fog has lifted. Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Meta, and other giants now dominate foundation models while Harvey leads legal and Cursor rules coding. Customer support converges on Decagon and Sierra and Perplexity stakes search. Capital and compute now block new LLM entrants, yet openings remain in accounting, compliance, and security. The next breakout will hinge on bold go to market, agentic workflows, and roll‑up strategies.

articles.title: FIGMA'S IPO ISN'T FLASHY — BUT THAT'S THE POINT
articles.summary: Figma just set its IPO range at $25–$28 a share, valuing the company at around $16B—less than Adobe once offered, but above its 2024 secondary round. It's not a moonshot, but it's solid: $1B run rate, strong margins, and a clear growth story. While most coverage focuses on past performance, Figma is quietly packing AI features into its product, possibly adding upside for retail investors who still believe in narrative.

articles.title: HOW CLAUDE IS BUILDING A UNIQUE GROWTH ENGINE OUT OF USER-CREATED APPS
articles.summary: Claude launched AI-powered apps with a clever twist - users pay with their own Claude subscription, not the creator's. A teacher can build a SAT prep tool and share it with students, but each student must sign up for Claude to use it. No API keys, no hosting costs, no authentication headaches. The constraint becomes the opportunity. Each app spawns its own growth loop as project tools drive team invites and calculators spread through forums. While Cursor and Bolt chase developers, Claude went the opposite direction - maximum constraints for minimum friction, turning every creator into a distribution channel.

articles.title: THIS EMAIL STRATEGY CHANGED EVERYTHING
articles.summary: Citizen, a real time safety alerts app, was losing users after it muted push notifications. Julia Knight tackled the issue by hand-collecting local emergencies, dropping headlines into a Figma template, and blasting fifteen tailored emails daily. The clicks proved so strong that the team automated triggers that now send forty million incident emails each month, bringing dormant accounts back for free, cutting paid acquisition, and even lowering iterable costs through renegotiation.

articles.title: ANTI-SPACE
articles.summary: Antispace is an AI operating system that predicts, coordinates, and executes actions across your workspace to automate repetitive tasks.

articles.title: HYPERARC
articles.summary: HyperArc is an AI-native business intelligence platform that delivers instant, auditable insights from natural language queries.

articles.title: FUNDS THAT INVEST IN FEMALE FOUNDERS (RESOURCE)
articles.summary: A list of funds that invest in female founders.

articles.title: SIX WAVES OF SOFTWARE PRICING
articles.summary: In the 1950s, your software arrived in a semi‑truck bolted to its hardware. Seventy‑five years and six pricing waves later, vendors bill for shelf‑less shelfware, meter compute like electricity, and blend seats with usage in hybrid plans. This author traces the journey from boxed SKUs to SaaS, the ASP flame‑out, CFOs fighting surprise overages, and why clear value metrics will decide how we price AI next.

articles.title: THE AI‑NATIVE ENTERPRISE PLAYBOOK
articles.summary: Now that every enterprise tool claims to be "AI-powered," what actually moves the needle? This playbook keeps it simple: pick a weird industry with painful, overlooked problems. Build for real workflows, not just demo decks. Prove your product works with clear, objective results, especially in edge cases. Let AI power more than just the product: use it across onboarding, support, sales, and GTM.

articles.title: HOW CARTA'S INTERNAL AI AGENTS SAVE THOUSANDS OF HOURS OF BACK-AND-FORTH WORK
articles.summary: Most teams use AI to save money. Carta used it to save time where it mattered most: judgment-heavy accounting tasks that bottlenecked client service. Instead of replacing humans, it built in-house agents that could gather context, reason through complex workflows, and act autonomously. One agent now handles 25,000 monthly tasks in seconds, saving 3,500+ hours.

articles.title: YOUR STARTUP WILL DRIVE ITSELF
articles.summary: Picture an AI growth hacker that boosts clicks in days while accidentally killing sign‑ups.

articles.title: CHARTA HEALTH RAISES $22M SERIES A TO REBUILD THE BACKBONE OF HEALTHCARE OPERATIONS WITH AI
articles.summary: Charta Health raised $22M in Series A funding led by Bain Capital Ventures to enhance healthcare operations with AI.

articles.title: PATTERNS ACROSS 5 YEARS OF YC INVESTING
articles.summary: YC investments have increasingly focused on cybersecurity, industrial/manufacturing, and B2B sectors since 2020.
